origin.name: Malta
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Public Registry (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Malta travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Valletta guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Malta is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Public Registry (English language)
special_rules: Malta is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member.
English is an official language in Malta, so death certificates are issued in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
The Magistrate's Court is involved in all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Maltese authorities is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Maltese death certificates are in English, which simplifies the process. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Valletta can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Malta to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Malta. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. English-language death certificates and apostille simplify the process. Contact us 24/7.
